checks.a_analysis: The HTML output faithfully replicates the visual organization of the source PDF. The single-column flow is maintained, and complex layout elements like the Strategic Direction goals (pages 14-16) are successfully converted into structured tables that preserve the logical relationship between the 'Need' and the actions taken. The overall visual organization is consistent with the source document's intent.

All tables in the document, including Table 1 (Required attributes for Seamless Parcels, page 31) and Table 2 (Outreach presentations, page 40), are correctly rendered as HTML tables with clear column alignment, visible borders, and distinct headers. The information is easy to read and logically structured. The stylized goal boxes from the strategic section are also rendered as clean, functional tables.

All meaningful images from the source PDF are present in the HTML output. This includes logos (NCGICC), diagrams (Figures 2 and 3), photos (Figures 1, 5, 12, 13, 16, 27), and numerous screenshots/maps (Figures 4, 6-11, 14-15, 17-26, 28-32). Every figure mentioned in the text and shown in the PDF has a corresponding image in the rendered HTML.

There is no evidence of body content duplication in the rendered HTML. Sections and paragraphs appear only once in the sequence defined by the source PDF. Navigation-related repetitions (headings in the TOC versus headings in the body) are expected accessible design features and do not constitute content defects.
